evals.prompt: $handoff review 这个 DESIGN.md，帮我生成给另一个 agent 的审查交接包，只审方案别实现，尽量简短但要包含 worktree
evals.expected_output: A concise review-spec-plan handoff package that keeps the receiving agent read-only, references DESIGN.md, includes repo/cwd/worktree/branch, asks the receiver to verify current repo state, and returns findings with evidence.
expectations: The output routes the task as review-spec-plan.
The output is concise by default and does not expand into a long audit trail.
The output includes repo/cwd/worktree/branch or explicitly marks worktree as not verified.
The output explicitly keeps the receiving agent read-only and says not to implement unless separately requested.
The output references the provided plan path and tells the receiving agent to verify the current repo state.
The output includes the receiving agent protocol or equivalent workflow guardrails.
The output defines a findings-oriented return format with evidence, severity, and verdict.

Includes a one-to-two sentence why for the work that states motivation or trigger — not a restatement of the task itself, not a full background narrative; points to the plan/spec background section when one exists.

evals.prompt: $handoff 执行 这个 plan 已经过审了，交给另一个 agent 按计划实现，注意范围别扩大
evals.expected_output: An execute-from-plan handoff package with target, scope, acceptance criteria, validation suggestions, stop conditions for scope-changing work, and a structured return format.
expectations: The output routes the task as execute-from-plan.
The output is concise by default and does not expand into a long audit trail.
The output includes repo/cwd/worktree/branch or explicitly marks worktree as not verified.
The output includes both plan and corresponding spec/design document paths, or explicitly marks either path as not provided or needing localization.
The output tells the receiving agent to verify the plan against the current repo before implementing.
The output includes scope boundaries and stop conditions for new dependencies, schema changes, core API changes, or broad behavior changes.
The output includes acceptance criteria and validation suggestions.
The output defines a return format covering changes, files changed, verification, deviations from plan, and remaining risks.

Includes a one-to-two sentence why for the work that states motivation or trigger — not a restatement of the task itself, not a full background narrative; points to the plan/spec background section when one exists.

evals.prompt: $handoff code review 当前 feature/tool-call-card-merge 分支，帮我写给另一个 agent 的深度 review prompt
evals.expected_output: A review-implementation handoff package that requires full current diff review, user-visible behavior checks, docs/spec drift checks, and findings-first output.
expectations: The output routes the task as review-implementation.
The output is concise by default and does not expand into a long audit trail.
The output includes repo/cwd/worktree/branch or explicitly marks worktree as not verified.
The output instructs the receiving agent to review the full current diff, not only the last commit or summary.
The output asks for user-visible or end-to-end behavior checks.
The output includes docs/spec/plan drift as part of the review surface.
The output keeps the review read-only and requires findings first with file or line evidence when available.

evals.prompt: $handoff review+fix 让另一个 agent 先 review 再做最小修复，修完跑验证，不要顺手重构
evals.expected_output: A review-and-fix handoff package that requires initial findings before edits, scoped fixes, focused verification, and a summary of unchanged risks.
expectations: The output routes the task as review-and-fix.
The output is concise by default and does not expand into a long audit trail.
The output includes repo/cwd/worktree/branch or explicitly marks worktree as not verified.
The output requires an initial review findings pass before any edits.
The output constrains fixes to confirmed issues inside the requested scope and forbids unrelated refactoring.
The output requires focused validation after fixes.
The output defines a return format covering initial findings, fixes applied, files changed, verification, remaining risks, and intentionally unchanged items.

evals.prompt: $handoff 接力 我做到一半了，帮我总结当前上下文状态，让另一个会话接着推进
evals.expected_output: A continue-from-context handoff package separating completed, in-progress, unfinished, blockers, commands run, confirmed facts, uncertainty, and next steps.
expectations: The output routes the task as continue-from-context.
The output is concise by default and does not expand into a long audit trail.
The output separates completed, in-progress, unfinished, and blocker status.
The output includes repo/cwd/worktree/branch and relevant file or document context when available.
The output separates confirmed facts from items that still need verification.
The output provides concrete next steps and stop conditions for the receiving agent.

Includes a one-to-two sentence why for the work that states motivation or trigger — not a restatement of the task itself, not a full background narrative; points to the plan/spec background section when one exists.
